script: Before forests. Before apples. Before squirrels had anywhere to hide nuts.
The first sharks showed up more than four hundred million years ago.
Trees took their time. They didn’t really arrive until tens of millions of years later.
So when you look at a shark, you’re not just looking at an animal.
You’re looking at a survivor from a world before forests.

image_prompts.setting_type: prehistoric ocean + barren shoreline, not museum
image_prompts.status: KEEP current v15 hold as style reference, but treat as one immersive scene, not proof of all-shot museum direction
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 premium natural-history miniature cinematic frame: calm ancient shark fully submerged in clean teal prehistoric water, waterline ripples and particles, distant barren volcanic coastline above/behind it with absolutely no trees, no grass, no plants, no forest, warm amber sun rays, macro depth, tactile matte textures, clean negative space for caption, awe not attack, parent-trusted science short
image_prompts.negative_prompt: trees, forest, grass, leaves, kelp, coral, plants, modern objects, museum display shelves, glass case, cracked dry lake bed, shark on top of water, toy shark, scary teeth, gore, generated text, logos

image_prompts.setting_type: museum/fossil proof; museum allowed here only
image_prompts.status: KEEP v16/v17 as possible proof holds; do not extend this setting to the whole video
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 premium natural-history fossil proof close-up: ancient shark tooth or fossil impression on dark stone slab under a single warm exhibit spotlight, teal shadows, dust motes, elegant macro photography, simple composition with empty caption space, tactile stone/paper-clay miniature feel, no readable text in image
image_prompts.negative_prompt: full museum hallway, repeated display cases, readable labels, busy shelves, scary shark mouth, gore, generated text, logos

image_prompts.narration: Before forests. Before apples. Before squirrels had anywhere to hide nuts.
image_prompts.setting_type: controlled tabletop comparison OR barren land with symbolic props; not another generic museum room
image_prompts.status: REWORK. v20 is only a fallback/proof-of-control hold; next pass must make one apple + one squirrel + one nut/acorn unmistakable and visually different from fossil shot
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 premium tactile miniature comparison scene on barren prehistoric rock beside shallow teal water: three clear future-things presented as absent/not-yet icons — one bright red apple, one small cute squirrel silhouette/miniature, one oversized brown acorn/nut — hovering or staged as ghosted future props above empty lifeless land, no trees, no grass, no leaves, no forest, warm spotlight accents, playful but high-end natural-history tone, clean caption space
image_prompts.negative_prompt: trees, branches, leaves, grass, forest, multiple squirrels, many apples, unclear nut, readable text, museum hallway, display shelves, clutter, gore, scary shark

image_prompts.narration: The first sharks showed up more than four hundred million years ago.
image_prompts.setting_type: graphic geologic time column, not animal portrait
image_prompts.status: NOT generated in current v15-v20 lane; make next after shot 03 reassessment if needed
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 premium geologic time visual: stacked rock strata/time layers rising upward like a beautiful cutaway column, tiny ancient ocean layer with shark silhouette near the deep bottom, later forest layer far above as a small delayed marker, teal-to-amber lighting, tactile paper/stone miniature, clean space for editor-added 400+ million years caption, no generated readable text
image_prompts.negative_prompt: shark portrait, museum hallway, readable numbers or letters, cluttered infographic text, modern city, gore, scary teeth

image_prompts.narration: Trees took their time. They didn’t really arrive until tens of millions of years later.
image_prompts.setting_type: immersive primitive shoreline/early forest, land is hero
image_prompts.status: NEEDS new generation; do not reuse barren no-plant prompt here because this is the first actual plant arrival beat
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 cinematic prehistoric shoreline miniature: strange primitive tree-like plants and early forest forms finally emerging on rocky land, land plants are the hero, ancient teal sea below/behind with a tiny subtle shark silhouette already present, warm low sun, tactile natural-history diorama quality, clean mobile-readable composition, wonder not clutter
image_prompts.negative_prompt: modern forest, apples, squirrels, modern flowers, tropical jungle, dense clutter, scary shark, gore, generated text, logos

image_prompts.narration: So when you look at a shark, you’re not just looking at an animal.
image_prompts.setting_type: aquarium/window POV; not museum proof table
image_prompts.status: NEEDS new generation; viewer perspective should make this distinct
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 premium modern aquarium viewing-window scene: small human/kid silhouette from behind looking through huge blue glass at a calm shark swimming past, faint reflection of ancient ocean/fossil texture layered in the glass, teal water glow, warm rim light on viewer, cinematic family science tone, clean caption space
image_prompts.negative_prompt: scary shark attack, open mouth, gore, crowded tourists, unreadable generated text, logos, museum hallway display cases

image_prompts.narration: You’re looking at a survivor from a world before forests.
image_prompts.setting_type: immersive ancient ocean callback; earned final shark hero
image_prompts.status: NEEDS final after style proof; can echo shot 01 but must feel like ending, not duplicate
image_prompts.prompt: Vertical 9:16 quiet epic ancient ocean wide shot: lone calm shark gliding through vast luminous teal water, distant barren pre-forest coastline above the waterline, no trees or plants anywhere, soft amber sun rays, drifting particles, premium natural-history cinematic miniature, emotional survivor payoff, spacious composition for final caption
image_prompts.negative_prompt: forest, trees, plants, grass, leaves, kelp, coral, many sharks, attack pose, scary teeth, gore, generated text, logos

motion_prompts.prompt: Slow underwater push with drifting particles and subtle caustics; shark remains stable and fully in water.
Almost-still proof reveal: soft spotlight sweep, dust motes, tiny camera push.
Physical-camera tabletop slide/rack focus across apple, squirrel, and acorn; no AI character wobble.
Editor-built vertical timeline/strata reveal; layers parallax upward, captions added in edit.
Slow reveal across primitive shoreline; plants enter as late arrival, shark tiny in background water.
Gentle aquarium-glass parallax: viewer silhouette holds still, shark glides subtly, ancient reflection shimmer.
Slow final drift in ancient water, particles and light rays, optional minimal shark glide only if stable.

sfx: gentle magical chime on 'older than trees' hook
low warm impact on final memory line
music_direction: Low-volume curious natural-history bed: underwater warmth, subtle museum/proof accents only on evidence beats, never making every shot feel like the same exhibit room.

visual_strategy_v21_reassessment.principle: Consistent quality, palette, lighting, and texture — not the same museum room every shot.
visual_strategy_v21_reassessment.overall_style: premium tactile natural-history miniature / cinematic educational frame; teal-blue water, warm amber highlights, macro depth, matte paper/clay textures where useful, clean mobile composition, parent-trusted science tone
visual_strategy_v21_reassessment.museum_usage_rule: Museum/exhibit language is allowed only for proof/evidence/comparison beats, roughly 20-30% of the short. Most beats should be immersive prehistoric scenes or literal comparison scenes.
visual_strategy_v21_reassessment.say_dog_see_dog_rule: Every narration beat must have a distinct literal visual payload before captions explain it. If the line says forests, apples, squirrels, nuts, timeline, viewer, or survivor, that visual noun must be visible or clearly absent.
